name: Rolled Veal Scallops with Proscuitto, Sage An
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, blend the softened butter, parsley, sage, garlic, and lemon peel until combined.
2. Spread the mixture evenly over each veal scallop.
3. Place proscuitto slices and fontina cheese on top of the butter mixture, adjusting to fit the scallops.
4. Starting at one end, carefully roll up each scallop in parchment or wax paper.
5. Tie each rolled scallop securely with kitchen twine.
6. Thread each roll lengthwise onto a 9-10 inch skewer.
7. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours or overnight to allow flavors to meld and the rolls to set.
8. Preheat the broiler.
9. Brush the rolls with olive oil before placing them on a baking sheet.
10. Broil, basting frequently with pan juices, until golden brown, about 8-10 minutes.
11. Remove the skewers and twine, arranging the rolls on serving platters.
12. Garnish with mint sprigs and lemon slices.
13. Serve with a sauce made from mayonnaise mixed with Dijon mustard and one finely minced garlic clove, flavored slightly with dry Marsala.
Note: Veal scallops can be substituted with turkey breasts for a delicious alternative.
